name: Don Fernando's Green Chile Stew
recipeIngredient: 1/2 lb Lean Ground Turkey or Beef (see note)
1/2 Cup Mexican Beer (Not Dark) (we use Corona)
2 1/2 Cups Poblano Peppers, Roasted, Peeled and Chopped (see note)
recipeInstructions:
(we use half this amount and seed the chiles)
1. Soak the beans in water overnight and cook until tender.
2. In a soup pot, sauté ground meat and sirloin in olive oil until fully cooked. Remove from heat.
3. Add chopped onions, garlic, and cilantro to the pot. Sauté for 3-5 minutes or until onions are softened.
4. Pour in chicken broth and beer. Return meat mixture to the pot. Bring to a boil, then reduce to a simmer.
5. Stir in poblano peppers, tomato, Tabasco sauce, oregano, cumin, parsley, salt, and pepper.
6. Simmer for 2 hours, stirring occasionally.
Note: You could eliminate the meat by using Textured Vegetable Protein (TVP) for the ground meat and doubling the amount of beans. I have not tried this combination yet.
